g.text: Bubble Fraction vs Micro-Batch Count for Pipeline Parallelism (PP) Degrees

div.p: 1F1B Bubble Fraction $f_{bubble} = \frac{P-1}{M + P - 1}$ Reduces bubble by ~50% vs GPipe

ul.li: 1F1B Advantage: Consistently achieves ~50% lower bubble than GPipe due to interleaved forward/backward passes.
Scaling Law: Higher PP degrees require linearly more micro-batches (M) to maintain the same efficiency.
Efficiency Threshold: For <10% bubble with 1F1B, target $M \ge 9 \times (P-1)$.
Memory Tradeoff: Increasing M reduces bubble but increases activation memory footprint.
